summary: Julia Evans gave a talk at NYC Python about using IPython Notebook for data analysis, using NYC's 311 calls dataset as a practical example. The talk explored real urban data questions like which borough complains most about rats (the Bronx) and which days have the most noise complaints (Saturday and Sunday). She shared the notebook for others to try themselves.
key_points: IPython Notebook is an effective tool for interactive data exploration and presentation
NYC Open Data's 311 calls dataset provides rich, accessible data for learning data analysis
Sharing notebooks enables others to reproduce and extend your analysis
money_quotes: We talked about which borough complains the most about rats (the Bronx) and which days of the week have the most noise complaints (Saturday, Sunday).
key_insight: Real-world public datasets like NYC 311 calls make data analysis tools like IPython Notebook immediately engaging and accessible to learn.
